## 68. Share Artifacts Between Jobs

**Sharing artifacts between jobs** means one job uploads a generated file, and another job downloads and uses that same file.

This is useful because each job runs in a fresh runner, so files from one job are not automatically available in another job.

## Why share artifacts between jobs?

✅ Build once and deploy later
✅ Share test reports
✅ Share `.jar`, `.war`, or build folders
✅ Avoid rebuilding same application
✅ Pass generated files between jobs

## Example: Build Job to Deploy Job

```yaml
name: Share Artifacts Between Jobs

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build Java Project
        run: mvn clean package

      - name: Upload Build Artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: java-app
          path: target/*.jar

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Download Build Artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: java-app
          path: app

      - name: Show Artifact File
        run: ls -la app

      - name: Deploy Application
        run: echo "Deploying application from app folder"
```

## Explanation

In the `build` job:

```yaml
uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
```

uploads the generated `.jar` file.

In the `deploy` job:

```yaml
needs: build
```

makes deploy wait until build completes.

```yaml
uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
```

downloads the artifact into the deploy job.

## Workflow Flow

```text
Build Job
   ↓
Create JAR file
   ↓
Upload artifact
   ↓
Deploy Job
   ↓
Download artifact
   ↓
Deploy app
```

## Real-Time Example

A Spring Boot project creates:

```text
target/myapp.jar
```

The build job uploads this JAR file as an artifact.
The deploy job downloads the same JAR file and deploys it to server.

## Best Practices

Use `needs` between dependent jobs.
Use clear artifact names.
Upload only required files.
Do not upload secrets.
Download artifact into a specific folder.
Use the same artifact name in upload and download.

## Simple Definition

**Sharing artifacts between jobs means uploading files in one job and downloading them in another job using GitHub Actions artifacts.**
